{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient developed hypotension after 3 minutes of administering 3% xylocaine with adrenaline via epidural anesthesia. Xylocaine is a local anesthetic that can cause systemic absorption if it enters the bloodstream. Adrenaline, when used in local anesthetics, can potentiate the effects of the local anesthetic, leading to faster onset and higher peak levels, which can increase the risk of systemic absorption. The sudden onset of hypotension suggests a rapid systemic effect, which aligns with the possibility of systemic absorption. Vasovagal effect is less likely to cause sudden hypotension, and an allergy to the drug preparation would typically present with immediate symptoms rather than delayed hypotension. Penetration into the subarachnoid space would more likely cause spinal anesthesia rather than hypotension.", "answer_choice": "A"}
